dependents.name: libnetplan1
dependents.runtime_functionality: Reads and writes Netplan YAML network definitions plus YAML state and patch data.
evidence: The Ubuntu binary package directly Depends on libyaml-0-2, and its package description says Netplan reads YAML network configuration files.
The netplan.io source uses yaml_parser_t and yaml_emitter_t in src/util.c and exports netplan_parser_load_yaml_* and netplan_state_*_yaml interfaces.

dependents.name: python3-yaml
dependents.runtime_functionality: Provides Python YAML parsing and emitting, with the packaged C accelerator backed by libyaml for faster load and dump operations.
evidence: The Ubuntu package description calls it a YAML parser and emitter for Python and says it is built using libyaml for improved speed.
PyYAML's yaml/_yaml.pyx wraps libyaml parser and emitter APIs for the accelerated code path.

dependents.name: ruby-psych
dependents.runtime_functionality: Implements Ruby YAML parsing, emitting, and object serialization through Psych, the default YAML engine in modern Ruby.
evidence: The Ubuntu package description explicitly describes ruby-psych as a libyaml wrapper for Ruby and says Psych is Ruby's default YAML parser.
ruby-psych's lib/psych.rb documents parsing, emitting, AST handling, and object serialization on top of libyaml.

dependents.name: php8.3-yaml
dependents.runtime_functionality: Adds PHP YAML 1.1 parsing and emitting functions such as yaml_parse* and yaml_emit*.
evidence: The Ubuntu package description says it is a YAML 1.1 parser and emitter for PHP using the LibYAML library.
The packaged source contains tests and metadata for yaml_parse* and yaml_emit* functionality.

dependents.name: suricata
dependents.runtime_functionality: Loads Suricata's YAML configuration, included YAML fragments, and tenant-specific YAML files such as suricata.yaml and tenant configs.
evidence: The Ubuntu binary package directly Depends on libyaml-0-2.
Suricata documentation states that Suricata uses YAML for configuration, centered on suricata.yaml, and the source tree has a conf-yaml loader plus multi-tenant YAML loading paths.

dependents.name: stubby
dependents.runtime_functionality: Parses the resolver's YAML configuration files such as stubby.yml at startup.
evidence: The Ubuntu binary package directly Depends on libyaml-0-2.

The getdns/stubby source ships a YAML example configuration, documents YAML configuration in stubby.1 and README.md, and includes a LibYAML-based YAML-to-JSON conversion path in stubby/src/yaml/convert_yaml_to_json.c.

dependents.name: ser2net
dependents.runtime_functionality: Parses /etc/ser2net/ser2net.yaml and also supports YAML-formatted controller input and output modes.
evidence: The Ubuntu binary package directly Depends on libyaml-0-2.
The ser2net source ships ser2net.yaml and ser2net.yaml(5), and controller.c uses libyaml document parsing for controller YAML mode.

dependents.name: h2o
dependents.runtime_functionality: Parses the H2O server configuration file, which is YAML, and also parses YAML-formatted TLS ticket secret files.
evidence: The Ubuntu binary package directly Depends on libyaml-0-2.
H2O's documentation says the configuration file syntax is YAML 1.1, and the source uses yaml_parser_t in src/main.c and src/ssl.c.

dependents.name: libcamera0.2
dependents.runtime_functionality: Parses camera YAML data at runtime, including camera_hal.yaml and multiple IPA sensor tuning files such as per-sensor .yaml calibration and tuning data.
evidence: The Ubuntu binary package directly Depends on libyaml-0-2.
libcamera contains a dedicated yaml_parser.cpp helper and calls YamlParser::parse() from camera HAL configuration code and IPA tuning-file loading paths.

dependents.name: libappstream5
dependents.runtime_functionality: Parses and emits AppStream YAML metadata, especially DEP-11 catalogs, and supports YAML-based NEWS and validator report handling.
evidence: The Ubuntu binary package directly Depends on libyaml-0-2 and describes itself as a library for reading, writing, and transforming AppStream metadata.

The appstream source includes YAML parsing and emitting code in src/as-yaml.c, src/as-metadata.c, src/as-news-convert.c, and compose/asc-compose.c, with AS_FORMAT_KIND_YAML documented as AppStream YAML (DEP-11) metadata.

dependents.name: crystal
dependents.notes: This is a compile-time and toolchain dependency rather than a direct runtime link to libyaml-0-2 in the packaged compiler binary.
evidence: The Ubuntu source package Build-Depends on libyaml-dev, and the binary package Recommends libyaml-dev.

The Crystal source tree includes stdlib YAML functionality, including YAML::Serializable support and YAML parsing and emitting code, which is why libyaml headers and development files are needed when building the toolchain.

dependents.name: libyaml-libyaml-perl
dependents.runtime_functionality: Provides Perl YAML parsing and emitting through YAML::XS, including direct access to the linked libyaml runtime version.
evidence: The Ubuntu binary package directly Depends on libyaml-0-2 and describes itself as a Perl interface to libyaml, a YAML implementation.
The YAML-LibYAML module exposes YAML::XS load and dump operations and a YAML::XS::LibYAML::libyaml_version() entry point backed by the linked libyaml library.
